notes: App boots into a playable level at '/'. Drive and verify via window.shelfRushQa (present in this qa build only). Cells are addressed as (compartment, cell); read getState().board to map shelves to products. NEW since the last package: levels 1-10 are a hand-authored curriculum (one new model per level), per-level star thresholds are seeded from the solver optimum (getState().level... use levelStars after a win), and the 320px objective strip no longer clips. Poll shelfRushQa.ready().ok before the first call (the container now binds at app root, so resetSave()/getState() work before any level loads - v3 P1.1). getState() also exposes suggestedMove (the hint booster's output) and score (3-star/2-star move thresholds). Levels 1-10 now show a player-facing teaching banner (the level's lesson) below the objective until the first move - watch for it at level start.

flows.name: level_1_guided_match
flows.description: Level 1's guided first move completes a triple (shelf 1 cell 0 -> shelf 0 cell 2).

flows.name: curriculum_walk_1_10
flows.description: Levels 1-10 are the new hand-authored curriculum, one new mental model per level: 1 first clear (guided), 2 second clear, 3 empty-space staging, 4 first hidden reveal, 5 first mystery bag, 6 first timer (45s), 7 hidden+staging combo, 8 bag+hidden combo, 9 timed 3-SKU sort (50s), 10 capstone (bag+hidden+4 SKUs, 75s). Each should load with a clear objectiveText and be completable cold. Levels 6, 9 and 10 report a timerSeconds; the rest are untimed (null).

steps.expect: getState(): status 'playing', a non-empty objectiveText, visibleProductCount > 0; timerSeconds is a number on 6/9/10 and null elsewhere

flows.name: level_4_hidden_reveal_completes
flows.description: Level 4's hidden-reveal sequence completes cleanly (v3 P0.2 regression). The reviewer's in-browser run stalled here under CPU-only CanvasKit; the move sequence is proven in CI and the bridge drives it deterministically.

steps.expect: clears the front triple and reveals 2 hidden products; visibleProductCount 6, status 'playing'

flows.name: mystery_bag_reveal_L5
flows.description: Level 5 introduces mystery bags. The product on shelf 1 cell 0 is wrapped in a bag; it can be selected and moved, and moving it opens the bag (the only blocker the engine clears through normal play). After the move its shelf can complete a triple. mysteryBag is used because tape/frost/crate blockers are hammer-only and intentionally deferred.

flows.name: three_star_seeded_P1_7
flows.description: Per-level star thresholds (levels 1-10) are seeded from the solver's minimum solution moves, so 3 stars is achievable and fair (the old par heuristic under-counted staging moves and could make 3 stars impossible). Complete a level within its optimum move count and getState().levelStars[levelId] should be 3; take a couple more moves for 2, more for 1.

flows.name: booster_no_consume_on_noop_P0_1
flows.description: An owned booster used where it cannot apply must NOT be consumed. Hammer needs a selected product; using it with nothing selected is a no-op, so the count is unchanged.

flows.name: replay_grants_no_coins_P0_2
flows.description: Re-winning an already-completed level grants no coins, and the win panel shows no coin row. Win a level, record getState (or the coin HUD), replay and win again; the coin balance must not increase the second time.
